title: Deity not remediless merely because it doesn't have voting rights: Madras High Court
description: The Madras High Court recently expressed dismay over the wilful disobedience of its earlier directions to remove encroachments from over 500 acres of land belonging to a temple in Karur district, remarking that the temple's deity cannot be left remediless merely because it lacked voting rights [A Radhakrishnan Vs P Madhusudhan Reddy].A Bench of Justices P Velmurugan and B Pugalendhi made the observation while dealing with a contempt petition alleging non-compliance with a 2019 order directing au
